assertions.reason: The launch flow now exposes a confirmation modal and then transitions the study to persona review, but it still never surfaces the raw `validateStudyLaunch { pass, reasons[] }` result described by the assertion.

The launch confirmation flow exposes only Cancel and Confirm Launch controls; there is still no visible production-ack control or way to submit a production launch without acknowledgement and observe rejection.
The launched study reached persona review, but visible variant generation never completed and no run detail reached a blocked_by_guardrail terminal state.
Cumulative-failure auto-cancel still requires real run execution with queued backlog, and the browser flow never progressed beyond persona review / generating variants.
Budget-cap enforcement still depends on admin settings changes plus live execution; the admin settings surface remains inaccessible from the browser.
guardrailEvents auditing remains a backend-table assertion and is still not directly observable from the browser surface.
Subsequent-study effects still require admin settings changes, but the browser surface never exposed an admin-capable settings session.

frictions.description: The study launch modal appeared even while the study was still in draft status.
frictions.resolution: Continued testing the visible launch flow to see which hardening behaviors were actually exposed.

blockers.description: Guardrail assertions that depend on admin settings, audit tables, or real blocked_by_guardrail run records remain unavailable from browser-only validation.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Imported and published a pack, created a fresh study, opened the launch modal, and confirmed launch, but the flow only advanced to persona review and then stalled on generating variants.

summary: The browser still only exposes high-level launch UI, not raw launch-validation payloads or blocked_by_guardrail run details.
